findings.our_description: Bilateral multisegmental, predominantly subpleural, reticular changes of the lung parenchyma with mosaic attenuation, air trapping, traction bronchiectasis. Findings appear stable from prior study. No definite honeycombing.
findings.final_description: There is peripheral reticulation with traction bronchiectasis throughout the lungs, most prominent in the upper lobes and without significant change compared to 11/3/2025. These findings are slowly progressing since 2023. No definite honeycombing.
findings.notes: Final added historical context: 'slowly progressing since 2023' by comparing with more remote priors (9/23/2024, 4/21/2023). Our report only compared to the most recent prior (11/3/2025). Also, final specified upper lobe predominance more clearly. This provides important clinical context about disease trajectory but does not change immediate management.
findings.mips_rationale: Discrepancy is about ILD characterization and historical trajectory context; no MIPS measure applies to pulmonary fibrosis documentation.

findings.our_description: Enlarged subaortic lymph node up to 1.1 cm in short axis (previously 1.0 cm). New enlarged prevertebral lymph node up to 1.1 cm in short axis.

findings.notes: Differences in lymph node characterization: We called one 'subaortic', final called it 'aortopulmonary window' (same anatomic region, different naming). We reported a 'new enlarged prevertebral lymph node'; final instead reported 'similar enlarged subcarinal node' and 'mildly enlarged pretracheal and mediastinal nodes without significant change.' The attending provided a more nuanced characterization of multiple node stations and assessed them as without significant change rather than new. Minor discrepancy in node station identification.
findings.mips_rationale: Discrepancy is about lymph node station naming and characterization of stability vs new finding; this is a clinical interpretation difference, not a MIPS measure.

findings.anatomical_location: left lower lobe and left upper lobe
findings.our_description: No pulmonary nodules requiring follow-up per Fleischner Society recommendations.
findings.final_description: AI-detected GGO nodules: LLL pure GGO 7.5 mm (8.8×6.1 mm, series 5/image 155); LUL pure GGO 4.0 mm (4.3×3.6 mm, series 5/image 149). Impression: GGO/nodules may represent infectious/inflammatory or adenocarcinoma spectrum; follow-up Fleischner 3-6 months; adenocarcinoma cannot be excluded.
findings.management_impact: We stated 'no nodules requiring follow-up'; final identifies a 7.5 mm pure GGO nodule requiring CT follow-up in 3-6 months per Fleischner guidelines and raises adenocarcinoma concern. Direct management change: follow-up CT ordered vs none.
findings.notes: Most significant discrepancy in this study. The 7.5 mm GGO nodule in LLL is above the 6 mm Fleischner threshold for multiple pure GGO nodules (CT at 3-6 months recommended). We completely missed this finding and explicitly stated no nodules required follow-up. The LUL 4.0 mm GGO is below threshold but the LLL 7.5 mm is clearly actionable. Grade 3: missed finding that changes patient management.

findings.final_description: Lower lobe predominant peripheral interlobular septal thickening with associated honeycombing suggestive of UIP pattern.
findings.management_impact: Adds UIP characterization; clinically meaningful context for ILD but does not independently change management beyond what was already recommended for ILD.
findings.notes: We described the same ILD findings (reticulations, traction bronchiectasis, ground glass) but did not use the term honeycombing/UIP pattern. The attending explicitly calls it UIP pattern. This refines the ILD diagnosis — does not change immediate management but is clinically meaningful.
findings.mips_rationale: Discrepancy is about UIP/honeycombing pattern specification the attending added; no MIPS measure applies to ILD pattern documentation.

findings.our_description: New peribronchial area of consolidation with ground glass around the periphery, series 5 images 82-92.
findings.final_description: New peribronchial area of masslike consolidation with central cavitation and peripheral ground glass attenuation, series 5 images 82-92.
findings.management_impact: Characterization as 'masslike with central cavitation' vs our 'consolidation with peripheral GGO' is a clinically meaningful distinction in the context of coccidioidomycosis but does not change management (both prompt follow-up; the attending's final impression still recommends 3-6 month follow-up same as implied by our report).
findings.notes: We noted 'pseudocavitation' for the adjacent second LUL consolidation (images 108-123) not for this one; attending calls this first lesion cavitary. The distinction between pseudocavitation and true cavitation has some clinical significance in infectious disease context.
findings.mips_rationale: Discrepancy is about cavitation characterization (pseudocavitation vs true cavitation) of a LUL consolidation; this is a clinical finding difference, not a MIPS measure.

findings.our_description: Subpleural area of consolidation, probably with pseudocavitation, series 5 images 108-123 (no interval change).
findings.final_description: Subpleural area of consolidation, probably with central cavitation, series 5 images 108-123 (no interval change).
findings.notes: Pseudocavitation vs central cavitation — same clinical meaning in context of stable known coccidioidomycosis lesion.

findings.our_description: Multiple new nodules: RML 3mm image 86, RLL 4mm with cavitation image 120, LUL 4mm images 90-95, LLL up to 6mm images 96-99. Calcified granulomas stable.
findings.final_description: Same nodules listed with 'New' prefix labels added. Calcified granulomas stable.
findings.notes: We used 'previously not visualized'; attending used 'New' prefix. Same clinical content.

findings.final_description: Watchman device identified within the left atrial appendage.
findings.management_impact: We identified 'mitral valve prosthesis'; attending identified Watchman LAA occlusion device — these are different devices. Missing the Watchman is a clinically meaningful omission (relevant to anticoagulation management and procedural history) but does not change immediate CT management.
findings.notes: We noted 'Mitral valve prosthesis' which the final does not repeat. These appear to be two different prosthetic devices — the Watchman is an LAA occluder, not a mitral prosthesis. It is unclear if the mitral prosthesis was missed by the attending or if this is an error in our report. Grade 2b for the missed Watchman device.
findings.mips_rationale: Discrepancy is about a Watchman device the attending identified; no MIPS measure applies to cardiac device documentation.

findings.our_description: Reduced attenuation of the intracardiac blood within the left ventricle, associated with anemia.
findings.management_impact: Attending dropped this finding; we described it and the attending chose not to include it. No management impact.
findings.notes: We included this finding; the attending omitted it from the final. Grading from attending's perspective this is not in the final — but since we reported it and they didn't, this is an attending omission not ours. From our quality perspective this is fine — we reported what we saw.
findings.mips_rationale: Discrepancy is about a finding we included (reduced LV attenuation suggesting anemia) that the attending omitted; no MIPS measure applies.

findings.final_description: Recommend follow-up in 3-6 months to ensure resolution of coccidioidomycosis changes.
findings.management_impact: Explicit follow-up timeline (3-6 months) added by attending. Our report implied follow-up by describing the changes but did not give a specific timeline. This adds actionable management guidance.
findings.notes: We implied surveillance was needed but gave no specific interval. Attending specifies 3-6 month follow-up. This is a meaningful clinical addition.
findings.mips_rationale: Follow-up recommendation for coccidioidomycosis resolution is not part of any applicable MIPS measure.

findings.final_description: Moderate centrilobular emphysema in both lungs.

findings.our_description: Consolidation significantly decreased compared to June 19, 2025 and September 19, 2025. CT findings favor postinflammatory changes in the right upper lobe.
findings.final_description: Nodular scar in the right upper lobe with associated traction bronchiectasis and a more solid nodular component measuring 2.5×1.1 cm, grossly stable to prior (5/62). Large consolidation on June 19, 2025 significantly resolved.
findings.management_impact: Attending specifies a residual solid nodular component of 2.5×1.1 cm with traction bronchiectasis — this is more detailed characterization of a residual solid mass. The size specification and 'stable to prior' context are clinically meaningful but do not change immediate management.
findings.notes: We described the consolidation as improving/post-inflammatory. Attending characterizes a specific residual nodular component with measurements. This is a more precise description of the residual finding.
findings.mips_rationale: Discrepancy is about more precise characterization of a residual nodular component vs post-inflammatory changes; this is a clinical finding difference, not a MIPS measure.

findings.our_description: Multiple centrilobular nodules, predominantly in the upper lobes, up to 4 mm, some with pseudocavities (series 5, images 76, 78, 137), without significant change.
findings.final_description: Multiple centrilobular nodules, predominantly in the upper lobes, up to 7 mm, some with pseudocavities (series 5, images 76, 78, 137), without significant change.
findings.management_impact: Size discrepancy: we said up to 4 mm; attending says up to 7 mm. A 7 mm centrilobular nodule warrants Fleischner follow-up; a 4 mm nodule generally does not in low-risk patients. Attending adds Fleischner 12-month follow-up recommendation based on the 7 mm size — this is a management difference.
findings.notes: The perifissural nodule up to 7 mm described separately in our report may be the source of the 7 mm measurement. Attending may be combining the two populations. Regardless, the size discrepancy affects the Fleischner recommendation.
findings.mips_rationale: Discrepancy is about nodule size measurement (4 mm vs 7 mm) affecting Fleischner recommendation; the underlying finding characterization differs, not just documentation.

findings.our_description: Perifissural nodules up to 7 mm along the right minor fissure, likely representing a lymph node.
findings.notes: We described this separately; attending likely absorbed into centrilobular nodule description or considered it the same population. Attending's characterization includes up to 7 mm nodules with Fleischner follow-up. Not clinically significant as a standalone omission.

findings.our_description: Calcified nodule up to 7 mm in diameter (healed granuloma) in right middle lobe.
findings.final_description: Right middle lobe, calcified nodule measures 7 mm in diameter (healed granuloma).
Follow-up according to Fleischner guidelines in 12 months.
findings.management_impact: Attending adds specific 12-month Fleischner follow-up recommendation. Our report did not include this recommendation for the centrilobular nodules. This is actionable management guidance we omitted.
findings.notes: Tied to the size interpretation difference (7 mm vs our 4 mm). Attending's recommendation is internally consistent with the 7 mm sizing.
findings.mips_rationale: The attending's Fleischner recommendation is directly tied to a different nodule size interpretation (7 mm vs our 4 mm) — this is a clinical discrepancy (sizing disagreement) that drives the different follow-up, not a MIPS documentation gap on top of an otherwise concordant finding.

findings.anatomical_location: bilateral bronchi — lower lobes, middle lobe, lingula

findings.final_description: Bilateral diffuse mosaic groundglass attenuation representing air trapping versus perfusional abnormality. Severe bronchial wall thickening, subsegmental bronchi obstructed by contents, some calcified. Numerous foci of mucous stasis.

findings.final_description: Cluster of bronchocentric tree-in-bud groundglass nodules in right upper lobe may represent active infection/inflammation (3/27).

Our report described centrilobular nodules generally but did not call out this specific RUL cluster as potentially active. This is a meaningful diagnostic nuance.

findings.mips_rationale: Discrepancy is about specific identification of tree-in-bud GGNs as possibly active infection vs our general centrilobular nodule description; this is a clinical interpretation difference, not a MIPS measure.

findings.final_description: Paraseptal subpleural emphysematous changes along the anterior aspect of right upper lobe. Suggestion of pan lobar emphysema in upper lobes.

findings.our_description: Micronodules in both lungs up to 2-3 mm. Multiple centrilobular nodules and centrilobular ground-glass opacities in upper lobes.

findings.mips_rationale: Discrepancy is about nodule size characterization (up to 2-3 mm vs up to 6 mm) which affects Fleischner classification; this is a clinical finding difference, not purely a MIPS documentation requirement.

findings.final_description: Mild central bronchiectasis and bronchiectasis predominantly involving the lower lobes.

findings.mips_rationale: Discrepancy is about bronchiectasis the attending identified that our report did not mention; no MIPS measure applies to bronchiectasis documentation.

findings.final_description: Recommend follow-up according to Fleischner guidelines. Low-risk multiple <6mm: no routine follow-up. High-risk multiple <6mm: optional CT at 12 months.

findings.final_description: Recommend future follow-ups with dual phase chest CT (inspiratory/expiratory phase) to better assess air trapping vs perfusional abnormality.

findings.our_description: RLL perifissural node 7mm (no change, no follow-up). LUL solid nodule 3mm (no change). LLL calcified granuloma 6mm (no follow-up). Bilateral lung cysts ≤1.1cm (clinically insignificant). Impression: no follow-up needed for any nodule.
findings.final_description: Same nodule descriptions. Impression changed: adds Fleischner follow-up recommendation at 12 months for the nodules, and adds dual-phase CT recommendation for airway disease assessment.
findings.management_impact: Final adds 12-month CT follow-up recommendation for nodules (our report said no follow-up needed). Also adds recommendation for dedicated dual-phase inspiratory/expiratory CT for air trapping assessment.
findings.notes: Our impression stated no follow-up required for any nodule. Final changes to 12-month Fleischner follow-up. This modifies management (additional imaging scheduled) but for stable nodules — clinical significance is modest. Graded 2b as it adds imaging but does not suggest malignancy or urgent action.
findings.mips_rationale: Our impression explicitly said 'no follow-up needed for any nodule'; SimonMed changed this to a 12-month Fleischner follow-up — this is a genuine management change driven by MIPS_364 compliance overriding our clinical conclusion, and the additional dual-phase CT recommendation is a separate element layered on top.

findings.our_description: Mild ground-glass opacities present in both lungs (no change). Impression: may represent sequelae of prior inflammatory changes.
findings.final_description: Final findings describe 'upper lobe predominantly groundglass attenuation may represent air trapping, incompletely assessed on single phase exam.' Our impression attributed GGO to prior inflammation; final recharacterizes as possible air trapping requiring dual-phase CT.
findings.management_impact: Recharacterization from 'prior inflammatory changes' to 'possible air trapping/small airway disease' changes follow-up recommendation (adds dedicated dual-phase CT). Alters recommended imaging.
findings.notes: Different clinical interpretation of GGO. Final's interpretation (air trapping) triggers additional dedicated CT; ours (prior inflammation) did not. Graded 2b.
findings.mips_rationale: Discrepancy is about clinical interpretation of GGO (prior inflammation vs air trapping) requiring different follow-up; this is a clinical interpretation difference, not purely a MIPS documentation requirement.

findings.our_description: Cardiomegaly CTR 0.57. Mild pericardial effusion ≤6mm (previously not seen). Mild coronary artery calcification with PCP referral recommendation.
findings.final_description: Cardiomegaly CTR 0.57. 'Trace pericardial effusion, new to prior.' Coronary calcification mild with identical PCP referral text. Cardiomegaly and pericardial effusion only in findings, not listed as separate impression items.
findings.notes: 'Mild' vs 'Trace' for pericardial effusion is minor wording difference; 'previously not seen' vs 'new to prior' is equivalent. Cardiomegaly and effusion noted in findings in both; omitting from impression does not change clinical meaning as they appear in the report. CAC management recommendation identical.

findings.mips_rationale: Discrepancy is about mild gynecomastia the attending identified that our report omitted; no MIPS measure applies to gynecomastia documentation.

findings.anatomical_location: right upper lobe, right middle lobe, right lung
findings.our_description: RUL: previously not identified part-solid nodule 4mm (image 76), part-solid nodule 2mm (image 78). Few right lung micronodules unchanged. Impression: Follow-up CT 3-6 months to confirm persistence; if stable, CT at 2 and 4 years.
findings.final_description: RUL part-solid nodule 4mm (image 5/76, same finding). Final adds: RML solid nodule 4mm (image 5/116) — new nodule not in our report. Impression changed to Fleischner: part-solid <6mm no routine follow-up; multiple solid <6mm no routine follow-up (low risk), optional CT 12mo (high risk).
findings.management_impact: Our recommendation: CT at 3-6 months (due to part-solid nature). Final recommendation: no routine follow-up for part-solid <6mm. Different management — final is less aggressive follow-up based on updated Fleischner guidance for sub-6mm part-solid nodules. Additionally final identifies an RML 4mm solid nodule we did not describe.
findings.notes: Management changed from 3-6mo CT follow-up to no routine follow-up. Final adds an additional nodule (RML 4mm) we missed. The Fleischner guideline interpretation differs. Graded 2b — different follow-up schedule but neither triggers urgent intervention.
findings.mips_rationale: Our report already provided specific follow-up guidance for the part-solid nodules (3-6 months, then 2 and 4 years); SimonMed changed to a different Fleischner interpretation ('no routine follow-up'), but since our report already fulfilled the MIPS_364 substance (specific follow-up interval + modality), this is a clinical difference in guideline application, not a MIPS documentation gap.

findings.our_description: No cardiomegaly. Pericardial effusion ≤8mm. Severe coronary calcification.

findings.notes: Different cardiomegaly assessment — ours says no cardiomegaly, final says borderline/mild. Clinically minor but a documented difference. Graded 2b.
findings.mips_rationale: The primary discrepancy here is a clinical difference in cardiomegaly assessment (our 'no cardiomegaly' vs final 'borderline to mild cardiomegaly'); the CAC management recommendation was added inline to the findings section but our report had already documented severe coronary calcification — the cardiomegaly disagreement is the defining discrepancy, and the CAC management note is secondary formatting.

findings.mips_rationale: Discrepancy is about terminology difference ('aneurysmal dilation' vs 'dilation') for the ascending aorta; this is a clinical characterization difference, not a MIPS measure requirement.

This is a Grade 3 miss — failure to raise endobronchial mass as a differential changes management (no follow-up CT was recommended by A).
findings.notes: Critical miss. The increasing endobronchial density should have triggered a specific follow-up recommendation. A noted it was increasing in size but did not act on this finding appropriately.

findings.our_description: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased bone tissue density in vertebrae at T11-L2 level down to +113 HU, numerous rounded areas of low density in the vertebral bodies. findings.final_description: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased bone tissue density in vertebrae at T11-L2 level down to +113 HU, numerous rounded areas of low density in the vertebral bodies. Osteosclerotic lesions on the right at the 7th rib, on the left at the 8th rib, with no change. There is flowing anterior osteophytosis, consistent with diffuse idiopathic skeletal hyperostosis (DISH).
findings.management_impact: Attending added diagnosis of DISH (diffuse idiopathic skeletal hyperostosis) based on flowing anterior osteophytosis. This is a chronic benign condition; identifying it adds clinical context but does not change acute management.
findings.notes: DISH is a recognized incidental finding on chest CT and its identification is clinically appropriate. No urgent follow-up required.
findings.mips_rationale: Discrepancy is about DISH identification (flowing anterior osteophytosis) the final added; no MIPS measure applies to DISH documentation.

No follow-up imaging recommended as incidental renal lesions without suspicious features are likely benign.

findings.final_description: There is eventration of the diaphragms
findings.management_impact: We called bilateral Bochdalek hernias; attending called it diaphragmatic eventration. These are different diagnoses: Bochdalek hernias are true diaphragmatic defects with herniated contents; eventration is an elevated but intact diaphragm. The clinical difference is moderate — both are generally managed conservatively, but Bochdalek hernias may warrant surgical evaluation while eventration typically does not. No acute management change likely in this context.
findings.notes: The attending's characterization as 'eventration' without specifying hernias suggests a different interpretation of the diaphragmatic anatomy. This is a notable diagnostic difference but likely benign in management terms for this patient.
findings.mips_rationale: Discrepancy is about diaphragmatic anatomy interpretation (Bochdalek hernias vs eventration); this is a clinical interpretation difference, not a MIPS measure.

findings.our_description: GGNs: confirmatory CT 3–6 months, then CT at 2 and 4 years if stable. Solid nodules ≤4 mm: no follow-up (low risk) or optional CT at 12 months (high risk).
findings.final_description: Solid 6–8 mm: CT 3–6 months then 18–24 months. GGN multiple <6 mm: CT 3–6 months, if stable CT 2 and 4 years. GGN ≥6 mm: CT 3–6 months, subsequent management based on most suspicious. Multiple <6 mm pure GGN: usually benign, consider follow-up high-risk at 2–4 years.
findings.management_impact: B recommends CT at 3–6 months for the ≥6 mm GGN; A does not recommend this interval because A did not identify a ≥6 mm GGN. Difference is downstream of LLL nodule characterization discrepancy.
findings.mips_rationale: B provides structured Fleischner-compliant follow-up intervals per MIPS 364; A's recommendation is less structured but substantially covers the Fleischner intent for the nodules A identified.

findings.finding_category: Interstitial lung changes — impression and follow-up recommendation
findings.our_description: Bilateral interstitial changes with subpleural and basal predominance may represent interstitial lung abnormalities (ILA) — citing Fleischner Society 2020. No specific imaging follow-up recommended.
findings.final_description: Mild nonspecific interlobular septal and mosaic GGA predominantly lower lobes. No honeycombing or architectural distortion. Recommend dedicated dual-phase CT (inspiratory/expiratory phase).
findings.management_impact: B recommends dedicated dual-phase CT (inspiratory/expiratory phases) — additional imaging study. A makes no such recommendation.
